Passing tests so far: 1

The test calls: `quote({ customer: { yearsWithMHPCO: 0 }, items: [{ type: "sword", material: "steel", enchantment: 0, cursed: false }] })` and expects `{ premium: 105 }`.

The previous passing test: `quote({ customer: { yearsWithMHPCO: 0 }, items: [] })` expects `{ premium: 5 }`.

Implement the minimal code to make the failing test pass while keeping the previous test green.
Do NOT refactor — that comes in the next phase.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text:
Both tests pass with the minimal implementation.

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added a check for `items.length > 0` that adds 100 (the sword base premium) to the processing fee.
- **Result**: All tests now pass (2 passing, 22 todo)
- **Approach**: Minimal hardcoded value of 100 for any non-empty items array. No item-type lookup, no iteration over items, no per-item logic — just enough to differentiate "empty items" (5) from "one item" (105). Future tests for amulets, staves, etc. will force generalization.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_04-01-47_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
